purpose: Date range parsing and management using Temporal.Instant. Supports relative time strings (1d, 2h30m), flexible date formats, and CLI integration for command-line applications.
keyExports: parseRelativeTime — Parse relative time strings (1d, 2h30m, now, today)
DateRange — Single date interval with after/before boundaries
DateRanges — Collection of date ranges with merge/contains operations
dateList — Parse date range strings into array of definitions
dateRanges — Parse date range strings into DateRanges object
dateStringToInstant — Convert compact date strings (YYYYMMDD) to Temporal.Instant
dateRangeOptions — CLI option definitions for @epdoc/cliapp integration

keyPatterns.name: Relative Time Parsing
keyPatterns.description: Parse human-friendly strings like '1d12h30m', 'now', 'today' into Temporal.Instant

keyPatterns.name: Single Date Range Operations
keyPatterns.description: DateRange class for containment checks, overlap detection, intersection, union

keyPatterns.name: Multiple Range Management
keyPatterns.description: DateRanges class for managing collections, merging overlaps, serialization

keyPatterns.name: Flexible Date Parsing
keyPatterns.description: Parse compact formats (YYYY, YYYYMM, YYYYMMDD), ISO dates, relative times

keyPatterns.name: CLI Integration
keyPatterns.description: Pre-built option definitions: range(), ranges(), since(), until(), window()

architecture.flow: types.ts → relative-time.ts, util.ts → date-range.ts → date-ranges.ts → cli.ts → mod.ts
architecture.keyDesign: Built on Temporal.Instant for modern date/time handling. Supports both programmatic use and CLI integration. Boundaries are inclusive.

v1.0.0: Range boundaries are now inclusive (was exclusive)
dateList() signature changed to accept options object
New DateRange class for single intervals
Added parseRelativeTime for relative time parsing
